Meta.title: Numbers — CFCC Reimbursements
Meta.description: Snap church receipts now, submit reimbursement claims later.

Home.profileNudge: Your <link>profile</link> needs a name and mailing address — both are printed on the reimbursement form.

SignIn.tagline: CFCC expense reimbursements — snap receipts now, file the claim later.
SignIn.popupBlocked: Your browser blocked the sign-in popup. If you opened this from inside another app, tap the menu and choose “Open in Safari” (or Chrome), then try again.
SignIn.embeddedBody: Google sign-in may not work in in-app browsers. If it doesn't, open this page in Safari or Chrome — tap the menu (⋯ or the share icon) and choose “Open in Safari”.
SignIn.notConfigured: No sign-in method is configured. Set FIREBASE_API_KEY, FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN and FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID in the environment.

Claims.title: Claims
Claims.subtitle: Draft claims need review; generated claims are ready to print and sign.
Claims.emptyBody: Select receipts on the <link>Receipts</link> page and hit “New Claim”.
Claims.counts: {items} items · {receipts, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}}

Profile.title: Profile
Profile.subtitle: Printed onto the “Name” and “Address” lines of the reimbursement form.

PdfPreview.omitted: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — open the PDF receipt to view it.} other {# more pages not shown — open the PDF receipt to view them.}}

Shoebox.title: Receipts
Shoebox.intro: Drop receipts here as you go. Select some when you're ready to file a claim.
Shoebox.selectedCount: {count, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} selected
Shoebox.selectPrompt: Select receipts below to start a claim
Shoebox.readingCount: Reading {total, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} with AI…
Shoebox.emptyBody: Upload a photo or PDF of a receipt to get started.
Shoebox.step1: <step>1. Snap.</step> Photograph receipts the moment you buy.
Shoebox.step2: <step>2. Batch.</step> Later, when you're ready, select receipts and hit New Claim.
Shoebox.step3: <step>3. Verify.</step> Check every row against the receipt. Fix, split, or exclude items.
Shoebox.step4: <step>4. Print.</step> Download the filled CFCC form with receipts attached, sign, and drop it off.
Shoebox.processedNote: Already on a generated claim. You can still pick it if part of the purchase belongs on another claim.
Shoebox.prepareHintLocal: Uploads when you save. You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.prepareHintUploaded: You can edit the description on the card later.
Shoebox.quotaRingAria: Retrying in about {seconds, plural, one {# second} other {# seconds}}

Viewer.previewUnavailable: Preview unavailable — use ↗ to open the PDF
Viewer.omitted: +{omitted, plural, one {# more page not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.} other {# more pages not shown — use ↗ to open the full PDF.}}

ManualEntry.title: Enter this receipt's details
ManualEntry.intro: The AI couldn't read {name}. Type in what's printed on it so you can verify the row.
ManualEntry.summaryPlaceholder: e.g. Paper towels, snack variety pack, folding table

ImageEditor.title: Rotate & crop receipt
ImageEditor.introRestoring: Showing the originally uploaded image. Save to keep it, or Cancel to leave the current one.
ImageEditor.introLocal: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background.
ImageEditor.introStored: Straighten the photo and drag the box to trim away the background. Saving replaces the stored image.
ImageEditor.resetTitle: Reset to the originally uploaded image
ImageEditor.loadFailed: Could not load the receipt image
ImageEditor.saveDisabledTitle: Rotate or draw a crop first

Review.title: Review claim
Review.instruction: Check each amount against what you actually paid, pick a ministry, then check it off.
Review.removeDisabledTitle: This is the only receipt — discard the claim instead
Review.removeTitle: Remove receipt from claim (back to the Receipts page)
Review.editPhotoTitle: Rotate or crop this receipt photo
Review.aiFailed: ⚠ The AI couldn't read this receipt.
Review.revertConfirm: Revert this claim to draft? Only do this if you haven’t turned in the printed form yet. Rows become editable again, and the receipts leave “processed”.
Review.removeConfirm: Remove this receipt from the claim? Its rows are deleted and the receipt goes back to the Receipts page.
Review.discardConfirm: Discard this draft claim? Receipts go back to the Receipts page.
Review.fanOutSet: Set {label} on {count, plural, one {# row} other {# rows}}
Review.splitModeBody: This claim is set to use one ministry for every row.
Review.splitModeBody2: Switch the claim to multiple ministries to split this row.
Review.modeSwitchTitle: Use one ministry for the whole claim?
Review.modeSwitchBody: Every row will be set to {value} — the ministry most rows already use.
Review.modeSwitchBodyNoMinistry: Rows keep no ministry until you pick one at the top.
Review.modeSwitchUnverify: {count, plural, one {# verified row} other {# verified rows}} will need re-verifying.
Review.descPlaceholder: What’s this claim for? e.g. “snacks for the youth retreat”
Review.suggestTitle: Let the AI suggest a ministry & event from your description — you still apply it
Review.noConfidentMatch: No confident match — pick a ministry below.
Review.appliedEveryRow: Applied to every row — you still confirm each amount below.
Review.aiSub: Describe the claim in a sentence — I’ll pick the budget category & event.
Review.candidatesLead: A few of these could fit — which budget line?
Review.somethingElsePlaceholder: Add a detail — e.g. “it was VBS, not a retreat”
Review.pickManuallyEscape: None of these — pick a category below
Review.multiHint: Each row picks its own ministry below. Switch to “One ministry” to set them all at once.
Review.badgeTitle: Ministry applies claim-wide — set it at the top
Review.mergeBlockedTitle: Restore the excluded row before merging
Review.mergeTitle: Merge back into the row above (undo split)
Review.nudgeSetAbove: Set the ministry at the top, then verify →
Review.verifyTitle: Click to certify that description, ministry, and amount are correct.
Review.underSignatureFrozen: Under signature — packet and rows are frozen.
Review.revertConfirmSigned: Revert this claim to draft? Every signature collected so far becomes void, and approval starts over.

Esign.readyBody: Submit this packet for an electronic signature instead of printing it.
Esign.withdrawConfirm: Withdraw this submission so you can pick a different approver?
Esign.failClosed: Signing is disabled until everything checks out.
Esign.openApprovedPacketButton: 🖋️ Open approved packet — with approver's signature
Esign.notAttested: You can't sign yet — turn on signing on your <link>profile</link>, then get vouched.
Esign.submitIntro: You're asking for approval of this claim ({amount}). Place your signature on the form, pick who should approve it, and sign. If the claim changes later, your signature stops counting and approval starts over.
Esign.typedNameLabel: Type your full name to sign
Esign.consentEnglishNote: The legally binding consent text is the English version below.
Esign.intentAffirmation: I intend this to be my signature.
Esign.connectBody: Sign in with your church Google account to sign securely. You only need to do this once on this device.
Esign.connectBlocked: Your browser blocked the sign-in window. Tap “Connect signing” again to allow it.
Esign.bannerOk: ✓ Everything checks out — this is the genuine paperwork, unchanged.
Esign.bannerBad: ✗ Something doesn't check out. Don't sign — ask the person who sent this (details below for whoever helps you).
Esign.anomaliesHeader: {count, plural, one {# invalid event on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):} other {# invalid events on the ledger (kept visible, never hidden):}}
Esign.independentCheck: Independent check: download the certificate and run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs with the church's published root fingerprint.
Esign.signedDragHint: Signed. Drag your signature if you want it somewhere else.
Esign.holdTabHint: Press and hold the yellow tab to sign on the line.
Esign.appearsAfterTap: Your signature appears after you hold the yellow tab.
Esign.couldNotOpenDoc: Couldn't open the document to sign. {message}
Esign.signAbove: Sign above with your finger or mouse

Approvals.title: Approvals
Approvals.subtitle: Open a claim to check it and sign.
Approvals.rejectNeedsComment: Add a comment explaining the rejection

Identity.title: Electronic signing
Identity.switchOnBody: Members can enroll, submit, approve, and pay electronically.
Identity.switchOffBody: Off for everyone (the default). Existing signed records stay verifiable.
Identity.bootstrapIntro: You're the person who sets up electronic signing for this church. It starts switched off, so nothing changes for members until you turn it on.
Identity.notSetUp: Electronic signing isn't set up for this church yet — ask your administrator.
Identity.enrollIntro: Sign and approve reimbursements from your phone — no printing. You'll agree to sign electronically, draw your signature, and then two members confirm it's really you (in person, one quick scan).
Identity.pendingVouch: One last step: show this to two members (or one approver) in person. They open Vouch for a member, scan it, and confirm it's really you.
Identity.manualFallbackBody: Have your voucher pick you from the list on their Vouch screen and type this key fingerprint by hand:
Identity.consentTitle: Sign electronically instead of on paper
Identity.consentIntro: Please read this once — it says your electronic signature counts like an ink one, and that you can always go back to paper.
Identity.drawIntro: This is the signature that appears on the reimbursement forms — sign the way you would on paper, with your finger or the mouse.
Identity.redrawBody: Your new signature is used on everything you sign from now on. Nothing you've already signed changes.
Identity.allowlistTitle: Members who can use electronic signing
Identity.allowlistNote: People you allow can see the signing features and enroll, but they still need to be vouched for before they can sign. Removing someone hides signing from them; anything they already signed stays valid.
Identity.allowlistLoadFailed: Could not load the member list

Devices.newDeviceTitle: This looks like a new device.
Devices.newDeviceBody: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this device yet. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed.
Devices.newDeviceBodyGone: Your signing identity exists, but it isn't on this account anymore. Bring it over — no re-vouching needed unless you start over.
Devices.approveFromOther: 📱 Approve from a device you already sign with
Devices.keysRemovedEverywhere: Your signing keys were removed everywhere, so the only way forward is to start over below.
Devices.noneWorkBody: If your old devices are gone and you never printed a recovery sheet, your identity can't be recovered — that's what keeps it yours. Starting over creates a brand-new signing key: the same people vouch for you again, in person, and the moment they do, your old key stops counting on its own. Worried someone might use the old key before then? Tell your administrator and they can retire it right away.
Devices.startOverConfirm:
Start over from nothing?

This signs ALL your devices out and throws away your current signing key. You'll set up again from scratch and be vouched for again in person — which automatically retires the old key. Anything you already signed stays valid.
Devices.waitingIntro: Now pick up the device you already sign with. A message appears there — type this code into it:
Devices.waitingNote: Waiting for the other device… this page continues by itself once it's approved.
Devices.phraseWrong: That phrase didn't work — check the words and their order
Devices.approveHint: Only approve if it's yours and in your hands. Type the 6-digit code from its screen — that's what proves it's really that device.
Devices.devicesTitle: Devices that can sign as you
Devices.removeConfirm:
Sign “{name}” out of electronic signing?

It loses access right away. If the device might be in someone else's hands, tell your administrator in person too, so they can revoke your signing key.
Devices.lostHint: Lost one? Remove it here, then tell your administrator in person if someone else might have it — they can revoke the signing key itself.
Devices.recoveryRoot: Protect the church's signing anchor. If this device is lost with no recovery sheet, electronic signing has to be rebuilt from scratch for everyone.
Devices.recoveryNudge: One more safety net: print a recovery sheet and you can sign from a new device even if this one is lost.
Devices.sheetBody: This one-page sheet holds the 24 words that can bring your signing identity to a new device. Print it and keep it with your important papers at home. Anyone holding the sheet can sign as you — and nobody, including the church, can recreate it if it's lost.
Devices.noPrinter: No printer? Copy the words by hand
Devices.savedCheckbox: The sheet is printed (or written out) and stored somewhere safe.

Vouch.title: Vouch for a member
Vouch.onlyAttested: Only attested members can vouch. Enable signing on your <link>profile</link> and get vouched first.
Vouch.doneBody: They need two member vouches (or one from an approver) to become attested.
Vouch.beforeTitle: Before you vouch, confirm in person:
Vouch.bullet1: The person is physically with you right now.
Vouch.bullet2: This name and email really belong to them.
Vouch.bullet3: You scanned the QR from THEIR screen (not a forwarded link).
Vouch.rekeyBody: Usually that means a lost device. The moment they're vouched back in, the old key stops counting — everything they signed before stays valid. Be extra sure it's really them.
Vouch.manualLabel: Type their full key fingerprint (from their screen — the spoken 6-digit code is never enough):
Vouch.confirmLabel: I am with {name} in person and confirm this identity is theirs.
Vouch.scanIntro: Scan the candidate's QR right here with your camera. Can't use the camera? Pick them from the list and type their full key fingerprint (read from their screen; the short spoken code is not enough).
Vouch.orPick: Or pick them and type their fingerprint
Vouch.roleDialogFromTo: This changes their role from {from} to {to}.
Vouch.roleExplainMember: Members can prepare and sign their own reimbursements, but can't approve or pay others'.
Vouch.roleExplainApprover: Approvers can approve reimbursement claims for payment.
Vouch.roleExplainTreasurer: Treasurers can approve claims and also mark them paid.
Vouch.keyDialogBody: Do this only when a device that could sign as them was lost or stolen. Everything they've already signed stays valid, but the key stops working everywhere — they'll set up signing again and get vouched back in person.
Vouch.malformedLink: This vouch link is malformed — rescan the QR

VouchScan.aim: Point at the candidate's QR code
VouchScan.notVouch: That's not a vouch code — line up the candidate's QR code.
VouchScan.insecure: This device can't open the camera here. Use the list below instead.
VouchScan.denied: Camera access was blocked. Allow it in your browser settings, or use the list below.
VouchScan.startFailed: Couldn't start the camera. Use the list below instead.

Verify.linkInvalid: This verification link is not valid ({part})
Verify.checkedNow: checked just now, in this browser.
Verify.rootRelayed: root relayed by this server (verify the fingerprint in person)
Verify.packetMatch: packet bytes match the signed hash ({hash}…)
Verify.packetMismatch: packet bytes do NOT match the signed hash
Verify.anomalyCount: {count, plural, one {# ledger anomaly} other {# ledger anomalies}}
Verify.rosterOddities: {count, plural, one {# roster oddity} other {# roster oddities}}
Verify.independentCheckCert: Independent check: run scripts/verify-bundle.mjs against the certificate's embedded bundle with the church's published root fingerprint.

Errors.verifiedEmailRequired: A verified email address is required
Errors.receiptOnClaim: Receipt is part of a claim and cannot be deleted
Errors.previewPdfOnly: Preview is only available for PDF receipts
Errors.imageEditImagesOnly: Only image receipts can be rotated or cropped
Errors.receiptFrozenByClaim: This receipt is on a generated claim — revert that claim before editing the image
Errors.nothingToRestore: This receipt has no earlier version to restore
Errors.cropTooSmall: Crop region is too small to stay legible
Errors.claimFrozen: Claim already generated; line items are frozen
Errors.ministryRequiredToVerify: Choose a ministry before verifying this row
Errors.splitAmountsInvalid: Split amounts must be non-zero and sum to the original amount
Errors.nothingToMergeInto: No row above from the same receipt to merge into
Errors.restoreBeforeMerge: Restore the excluded row before merging
Errors.claimSettingsFrozen: Claim already generated; review settings are frozen
Errors.onlyDraftDeletable: Only draft claims can be deleted
Errors.receiptsNotFound: One or more receipts were not found
Errors.claimReceiptsFrozen: Claim already generated; receipts are frozen
Errors.receiptsAlreadyOnClaim: One or more receipts are already on this claim
Errors.claimEmpty: Claim has no line items to reimburse
Errors.rowsUnverified: {count} row(s) still need verification before the PDF can be generated
Errors.rowsMissingMinistry: {count} row(s) still need a ministry before the PDF can be generated
Errors.receiptNotOnClaim: Receipt not found in this claim
Errors.rowAlreadyEdited: This receipt's row has already been edited — adjust it directly
Errors.lastReceiptOnClaim: This is the only receipt in the claim — discard the claim instead
Errors.onlyGeneratedRevertible: Only generated or under-signature claims can be reverted to draft
Errors.descriptionRequired: Describe the claim in a sentence first
Errors.aiRateLimited: The AI provider is rate-limited right now — try again in a minute or pick a ministry manually
Errors.aiNoSuggestion: The AI couldn't produce a suggestion — pick a ministry manually
Errors.aiQuotaExhausted: The AI provider is busy right now — please wait a minute and try again. Affected: {names}
Errors.quotaWait: Rate limit reached — waiting {seconds}s before retrying ({attempt}/{maxRetries})…
Errors.pdfFrozen: The packet is frozen under signature — download it from the claim page, or revert to draft to edit

esign.pickApprover: Pick an approver and type your name
esign.placeSignature: Place your signature on the form first
esign.typeNameApproval: Type your name to sign an approval
esign.selfApprove: You cannot approve your own claim
esign.notApprover: That member is not an attested approver
esign.packetChanged: The packet changed since preflight — restart the ceremony
esign.generateFirst: Generate the packet PDF before submitting
esign.mirrorBehind: Ledger mirror is behind — reconcile this claim first
esign.noPacket: No packet for this claim yet
esign.certNotReady: Certificates exist once a claim is approved
esign.notSetUp: E-sign is not set up yet
esign.notAllowed: Electronic signing is not enabled for your account

AddReceipts.title: Add receipts to this claim
AddReceipts.intro: Pick from your existing receipts or upload new ones — each is read with AI and gets its own row to verify.
AddReceipts.emptyBody: Upload a photo or PDF of a receipt to add it.
AddReceipts.readingCount: Reading {total, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}} with AI…
AddReceipts.confirmCount: ✨ Add {count, plural, one {# receipt} other {# receipts}}
